name: coreboot verified boot
description: coreboot verified boot checks that signed firmware stages are loaded when the device starts. On systems that expose firmware storage as MTD this should be paired with MTD locking so the verified firmware storage cannot be modified from the host.
failure-impact: When verified boot is not enabled, or when the firmware storage is writable, an attacker may be able to run modified firmware stages.
resolution: Contact your OEM, who may be able to issue a firmware update.
